steps.prompt: Tap each part of the circle
steps.subtitle: A circle is more than one round line — each straight line on it has a name and a job. Meet all five: [radius](purple), [chord](green), [diameter](orange), [secant](blue), [tangent](red).
steps.doneNote: That's the core vocabulary — five lines you'll use all course.

targets.prompt: Tap the [radius](purple) — the line from the center O straight out to the rim.
targets.label: Radius
targets.hint: It starts at the middle dot O and stops at the edge — half of a diameter.

targets.prompt: Tap a [chord](green) — a straight line joining two points on the rim, like a guitar string across the hole.
targets.label: Chord
targets.hint: Both ends sit on the rim, but it skips the center.

targets.prompt: Tap the [diameter](orange) — the chord that runs straight through the center, rim to rim.
targets.label: Diameter
targets.hint: It's the longest chord — the one passing through the center O.

targets.prompt: Tap the [secant](blue) — a line that slices through the circle at two points and keeps going.
targets.label: Secant
targets.hint: Like a chord, but it carries on past both ends (note the arrows).

targets.prompt: Tap the [tangent](red) — a line that just grazes the rim at a single point, like a road touching a roundabout.
targets.label: Tangent
targets.hint: It touches the edge once and never goes inside.

steps.prompt: Sort each line by what it does
steps.subtitle: Tap a card, then tap its group. Tip: first check whether it reaches the middle dot O.

steps.prompt: Match each part to what it means
steps.subtitle: Tap a term, then tap its meaning — or the other way around.
steps.doneNote: Vocabulary locked in. Remember: the radius reaches halfway, the diameter all the way.

pairs.right: From the center out to the rim — reaches halfway across
Joins two points on the rim, like a guitar string across the hole
A chord through the center — reaches all the way across
A road that cuts through the circle at two points
A road that just touches the circle at one point

steps.body:
The [radius](purple) reaches halfway — center O out to the rim.
The [diameter](orange) is a [chord](green) straight through the center, so it spans the whole circle.
So the diameter is exactly twice the radius:
diameter = 2 × radius

steps.prompt: True or false? Tap through the deck.
steps.subtitle: One claim about each part — decide if it gets the definition right.

cards.statement: A diameter is a chord that passes through the center.
cards.note: Right — the [diameter](orange) is the special [chord](green) running straight through the center O, rim to rim. It's the longest chord of all.

cards.statement: A tangent crosses the circle at two points.
cards.note: No — a [tangent](red) just grazes the rim at a single point. The line that cuts across at two points is the [secant](blue).

cards.statement: A radius runs from the center out to the rim.
cards.note: Yes — the [radius](purple) goes from the center O straight out to the rim, reaching only halfway across the circle.

cards.statement: A chord must pass through the center of the circle.
cards.note: No — a [chord](green) just joins two points on the rim and can skip the center entirely. Only when it does pass through the center is it a [diameter](orange).

cards.statement: A secant cuts through the circle at two points and keeps going past both ends.
cards.note: Right — a [secant](blue) is like a [chord](green) extended: it slices the rim at two points and carries on past both ends.

cards.statement: The diameter is twice as long as the radius.
cards.note: Yes — the [radius](purple) reaches halfway and the [diameter](orange) reaches all the way across, so diameter = 2 × radius.

steps.doneNote: Deck cleared — you've got the five parts and how they size up. On to the real circle theorems.
